scenario.name: Quality anomaly correlated with Robot R-07 health drift
script: Start with all robots running normally.
Vision Agent detects a surface scratch on part BX-2041.
Robot Health Agent correlates defect with torque and vibration drift on Robot R-07.
Predictive Maintenance Agent estimates remaining useful life of 38 hours.
Knowledge Agent retrieves gripper alignment SOP.
Supervisor Agent drafts a high-priority work order and quantifies business impact.

defectEvents.label: Surface scratch
defectEvents.action: Hold batch and inspect gripper alignment

defectEvents.label: Edge dent
defectEvents.action: Increase sampling rate to 100% for next 25 parts

defectEvents.label: Coating discoloration
defectEvents.action: Notify quality lead and compare paint temperature profile

sops.title: Robot R-07 Gripper Alignment & Calibration
sops.trigger: Defect rate increase with torque variance above baseline
steps: Move cell to safe inspection state and pause infeed conveyor.
Lock out motion path and verify operator safety perimeter.
Inspect gripper jaw parallelism using 0.05 mm gauge.
Check end-effector mounting bolts and cable dress tension.
Run torque zero calibration routine from teach pendant.
Execute five-part validation cycle and compare defect confidence below 20%.
Log intervention notes and close work order after quality approval.
acceptanceCriteria: Torque variance returns below 8% of baseline
Surface scratch confidence below 20% for 20 consecutive parts
Cycle time returns within 3 seconds of takt target

sops.title: Vision Camera Recalibration for Conveyor Inspection
sops.trigger: False positive rate exceeds threshold or lighting drift detected
steps: Clean lens and verify LED light bar intensity.
Place reference calibration plate on conveyor.
Run calibration wizard and validate bounding box alignment.
Capture 10 golden samples and 10 known defect samples.
Approve calibration only if detection precision exceeds 95%.

sops.title: Servo Drive Temperature & Vibration Check
sops.trigger: RUL below 60 hours or temperature above 65°C
steps: Review trend of temperature, vibration and cycle time.
Inspect servo cabinet cooling and filters.
Check abnormal vibration at robot base and joint axis.
Compare alarm logs for repeated overload conditions.
Schedule planned service if RUL remains below 72 hours.

sops.title: Quality Batch Hold & Release Procedure
sops.trigger: High-severity defect detected on two or more parts in same batch
steps: Place current batch on digital hold.
Notify quality engineer and production supervisor.
Sample 15 parts upstream and downstream.
Link defect images to batch record.
Release batch only after root cause is marked resolved.
acceptanceCriteria: No repeat high-severity defect across validation sample

workOrders.title: Inspect gripper alignment causing surface scratches
workOrders.businessImpact: Avoids projected 2.4h downtime and ₹4.8L scrap exposure

workOrders.title: Calibrate Vision Arch VA-03 lighting profile
workOrders.businessImpact: Reduces false positive inspection holds by estimated 18%

workOrders.title: Preventive check on servo cabinet cooling
workOrders.businessImpact: Protects against unplanned thermal trip on Cell 5

contracts.name: Reference Implementation Scope - Industrial AI Command Center
contracts.summary: Covers a static reference implementation using synthetic robot telemetry, simulated vision feed, SOP knowledge base, and agentic workflow orchestration for client demonstration.
clauses: No customer production data is required for the demo.
Synthetic telemetry must be clearly tagged as demo data.
Production integration requires read-only access to shopfloor systems and SOP repositories.

contracts.name: Sample Maintenance Response SLA
contracts.summary: Defines response targets for critical robot cells, priority classification, and escalation owners.
clauses: High priority work orders must be acknowledged within 15 minutes.
Critical robot stoppage must be triaged within 30 minutes.
Quality-linked maintenance events require supervisor approval before closure.

knowledgeQuestions.q: Why is Robot R-07 producing defects?
knowledgeQuestions.answer: Robot R-07 shows rising torque variance, vibration above the 5.5 mm/s warning band, and increased cycle time. The most likely root cause is gripper alignment drift or tool wear. Run SOP-R07-GRIPPER-ALIGN-001.
Use SOP-R07-GRIPPER-ALIGN-001. It takes approximately 18 minutes and requires safe inspection state, gripper jaw parallelism check, torque zero calibration, and five-part validation cycle.

If unresolved, the simulated model projects 2.4 hours of downtime risk, 3.8 percentage point scrap increase, and ₹4.8L scrap exposure. Recommended action is a micro-stop inspection before the next production batch.
A high-priority work order should be created for Robot R-07 with the title: Inspect gripper alignment causing surface scratches. Link events D-1001 and D-1002 and attach SOP-R07-GRIPPER-ALIGN-001.
